fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: District of Columbia Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to District of Columbia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity district of columbia, how to transfer audiologist license to district of columbia, audiologist compact district of columbia, district of columbia audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: DC Department of Health (DOH), Health Regulation and Licensing Administration
board.mailing_address: Health Regulation and Licensing Administration, 899 North Capitol Street NE, Washington, DC 20002
board.physical_address: 899 North Capitol St. NE, Washington, DC 20002

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in District of Columbia.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Register for a new account on the DC Health Licensing Portal (doh.force.com/dchealth/s/login/) if you do not have one already, providing all necessary personal information.

steps.title: Start Application for Endorsement
steps.description: Log in to the DC Health Licensing Portal and initiate a new application for Audiology licensure, specifically selecting the 'Application by Endorsement' option.

steps.title: Complete Online Application Form
steps.description: Fill out the entire online application form thoroughly, providing all requested personal, professional, and licensure history information as required for endorsement.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all required supporting documents directly to the DC Health Licensing Portal. This typically includes official transcripts, verification of all current/prior licenses in good standing from other states, professional liability insurance, and any other documents specified in the Audiology Application by Endorsement instructions.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee of $230.00 through the secure payment system within the DC Health Licensing Portal.

steps.title: Await Review and Approval
steps.description: Ensure any required criminal background check is completed if applicable and await review of your application by the Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology. You may be contacted if additional information is needed.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a District of Columbia audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in District of Columbia
hero.dek: Audiologists seeking licensure by endorsement in DC must apply through the DC Health Licensing Portal, which handles all professional licensing applications for the District.

official_links.registration_steps: Click 'Register for an Account' on the login page. Follow the prompts to enter your personal information, create a username and password, and complete the account setup process.
